arguments.command_line: cd /workspace && mkdir -p game-2048 && cd game-2048 && npm create vite@latest . -- --template react-ts

arguments.content: You are helping build a React 2048 game. First inspect whether there are any likely pitfalls in Vitest/Vite config for a Vite React TS app where production build must pass and tests are for pure game logic. Reply with concise recommendations only; do not modify files.

arguments.command_line: cd /workspace && rm -rf game-2048 && npm create vite@latest game-2048 -- --template react-ts
cd /workspace/game-2048 && npm install && npm install -D vitest
cd /workspace/game-2048 && find . -maxdepth 2 -type f | sort && echo '---vite.config.ts---' && sed -n '1,200p' vite.config.ts && echo '---package.json---' && sed -n '1,220p' package.json

arguments.content:
import { useEffect, useState } from 'react' import './App.css' import { applyMove, createInitialState, type Direction, type GameState } from './game' const keyToDirection: Record = { ArrowLeft: 'left', ArrowRight: 'right', ArrowUp: 'up', ArrowDown: 'down', a: 'left', A: 'left', d: 'right', D: 'right', w: 'up', W: 'up', s: 'down', S: 'down', } const statusText: Record = { playing: 'Keep going!', won: 'You reached 2048!', 'game-over': 'Game over — no moves left.', } function App() { const [game, setGame] = useState(() => createInitialState()) useEffect(() => { const onKeyDown = (event: KeyboardEvent) => { const direction = keyToDirection[event.key] if (!direction) return event.preventDefault() setGame((current) => applyMove(current, direction)) } window.addEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown) return () => window.removeEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown) }, []) return (

2048

Use arrow keys or WASD to combine tiles.

Score {game.score}

{statusText[game.status]}

{game.board.map((row, rowIndex) => row.map((value, colIndex) => (
{value === 0 ? '' : value}
)), )}
) } export default App

arguments.command_line: cd /workspace/game-2048 && npm test && npm run build
